title: A Pharaoh To Remember

jokes.text: More like three lockers and a sink.
jokes.setup: News reports contents of over three lockers stolen
jokes.punchline: Bender corrects them with unnecessary precision about his crime
jokes.explanation: Classic Bender - can't help but correct even when it incriminates him. Good character integration.

jokes.text: Holy Spitz! He's sporting skintight Speedos. - They don't leave much to be imagined. - On a robot, they do.
jokes.setup: Standard sexy Speedos comment
jokes.punchline: Robot anatomy makes Speedos pointless
jokes.explanation: Clean setup-punchline with good misdirection. Well-structured joke about robot anatomy.

jokes.text: Make me famous, bighead!
jokes.setup: Bender wants fame from his crime
jokes.punchline: Insulting the reporter while asking for positive coverage
jokes.explanation: Pure Bender logic - insulting someone while needing their help. Good character work.

jokes.text: It's Bender. Why bother remembering anything? You'll forget it five seconds later.
jokes.setup: Bender wants to be remembered
jokes.punchline: Fry immediately demonstrates the problem by forgetting Bender's name
jokes.explanation: Good character integration - Fry's stupidity perfectly illustrates Bender's point about being forgotten.

jokes.text: doomed to obscurity like the rest of you, especially Leela.
jokes.setup: Bender lamenting shared obscurity
jokes.punchline: Singling out Leela for extra insult
jokes.explanation: Classic Bender meanness - can't help but make it personal even when making a general point.

jokes.text: Boy, I've never seen him so down, or ever before.
jokes.setup: Sympathetic observation about Bender's mood
jokes.punchline: Reveals Fry doesn't actually know Bender
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry line - starts sympathetic but reveals his obliviousness. Excellent economy and misdirection.

jokes.text: I'm stuck, but I haven't given up hope! Call a soft-news journalist! - You're not stuck. - Shut up!
jokes.setup: Fry dramatically declares being stuck
jokes.punchline: Angrily rejects help because it ruins his drama
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Fry preferring drama to actual solutions. Absurd journalism reference.

jokes.text: This circle's about free expression not fascist moves!
jokes.setup: Bender trying to get famous with dance moves
jokes.punchline: Hippie takes dance move names literally as political oppression
jokes.explanation: Clever play on 'The Bender' as both dance move and political reference. Good hippie stereotype.

jokes.text: No one will forget how I live or my attitude regarding butt.
jokes.setup: Bender making grand statement about his legacy
jokes.punchline: Reduces to juvenile butt reference
jokes.explanation: Classic Bender - grandiose statement deflated by crude reference. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: Cushiony! And a minibar!
jokes.setup: Bender getting into his own coffin
jokes.punchline: Treating funeral like luxury hotel experience
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender - even at his own funeral, he's focused on luxury amenities.

jokes.text: crushed by a runaway semi driven by the Incredible Hulk.
jokes.setup: Professor describing Bender's fictional death
jokes.punchline: Absurdly specific and impossible cause involving comic book character
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist escalation - starts with mundane semi truck, adds impossible Hulk element.

jokes.text: Aw, you knew my favorite cause of death.
jokes.setup: Professor's elaborate death description
jokes.punchline: Bender treating death causes like preferences
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy - casual attitude toward death preferences. Perfect Bender characterization.

jokes.text: But Bender was different. Bender had a.04-percent nickel impurity. - It's what made me me!
jokes.setup: Professor giving technical eulogy about metal composition
jokes.punchline: Bender emotionally relating to manufacturing defect as identity
jokes.explanation: Excellent joke about individuality - reduces personality to manufacturing error. Great sci-fi comedy writing.

jokes.text: It's what made me me!
jokes.setup: Professor's technical description of Bender's impurity
jokes.punchline: Bender emotionally embraces this as his defining characteristic
jokes.explanation: Nice callback that shows Bender's ego extending even to technical specifications about himself.

jokes.text: If you needed a package brought into the country without x-raying... Bender always had a free body cavity.
jokes.setup: Amy giving eulogy tribute
jokes.punchline: Remembering Bender for smuggling services using his robot body
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about robot anatomy for illegal purposes. Good character callback to Bender's criminal nature.

jokes.text: You people look like you're waiting for the bus.
jokes.setup: Bender complaining about lack of emotion at his funeral
jokes.punchline: Specific mundane comparison to bus stop boredom
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about funeral mood. Relatable comparison everyone understands.

jokes.text: You're at my funeral, singing about some dead stiff named Danny-boy?
jokes.setup: Someone sings 'Danny Boy' at Bender's funeral
jokes.punchline: Bender is offended they're singing about someone else
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender narcissism - even at his own funeral he's jealous of attention given to others.

jokes.text: You really are a massive bonehead! - I'm expressing sorrow. - Get lost! I'd say, 'Don't quit your day job,' but you're bad at it too!
jokes.setup: Bender criticizing Fry's funeral singing
jokes.punchline: Escalates from performance criticism to general life criticism
jokes.explanation: Great escalation joke - classic insult format subverted with broader life failure. Good Bender cruelty.

jokes.text: You've convinced me life is worth living... by showing how bad my funeral will suck!
jokes.setup: Sounds like Bender learned positive life lesson
jokes.punchline: Reveals it's actually about his funeral being disappointing
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection - starts like genuine emotional growth, reveals selfish motivation. Classic Bender.

jokes.text: I thought something looked different in here.
jokes.setup: Massive sandstone block obviously missing from ship
jokes.punchline: Fry's massive understatement about obvious change
jokes.explanation: Classic Fry obliviousness - extreme understatement of obvious situation. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: Call it a hunch, but I've got a bad feeling about this.
jokes.setup: Just learned they're now slaves
jokes.punchline: Treating enslavement as mere 'hunch' about bad situation
jokes.explanation: Good understatement joke - treating obvious disaster as intuitive feeling. Classic sitcom structure.

jokes.text: That's the only thing about being a slave.
jokes.setup: Leela lists the bad things about slavery
jokes.punchline: Amy suggests those are literally the only problems
jokes.explanation: Clever deadpan - reduces slavery to simple checklist. Good economy and unexpected perspective.

jokes.text: That's the only thing about being a slave.
jokes.setup: Bender lists the bad things about slavery: no pay and no freedom
jokes.punchline: Fry says that's literally the only bad thing
jokes.explanation: Brilliant understatement that makes slavery sound minor. Dark humor with perfect Fry naivety.

jokes.text: This place is like the ancient Egypt of my day!
jokes.setup: Fry observing Egyptian-style civilization
jokes.punchline: Circular logic - comparing Egypt-like place to Egypt
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry stupidity - tautological observation that makes no sense. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: Insane theories: one. Regular theories: a billion!
jokes.setup: Professor's explanation of alien contact with Egypt
jokes.punchline: Amy keeping score of Professor's theory sanity ratio
jokes.explanation: Meta-joke about Professor's character trait. Good running gag setup with specific scoring format.

jokes.text: and how to prepare our dead so as to scare Abbott and Costello.
jokes.setup: Listing things learned from Egyptians including mummification
jokes.punchline: References classic comedy duo's mummy movies as mummification goal
jokes.explanation: Pop culture reference humor - relies on knowing Abbott and Costello mummy films. Somewhat cheap.

jokes.text: Also Wolfman.
jokes.setup: Already mentioned scaring Abbott and Costello
jokes.punchline: Adds random monster movie reference
jokes.explanation: Weak escalation - just adds another movie reference without connection. Lazy writing.

jokes.text: Spend your whole life building a guy's toe, you'll remember him.
jokes.setup: Discussion of monument building and memory
jokes.punchline: Bender's practical wisdom about labor creating memory
jokes.explanation: Smart observation about monuments and memory. Shows Bender's cunning beneath his crude exterior.

jokes.text: I noticed your sign. I thought I'd look into your program. - We make you starve to death.
jokes.setup: Bender treating slavery like voluntary program
jokes.punchline: Blunt description of slavery as death program
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy - treats horrific slavery like customer service. Great structure and economy.

jokes.text: Aw, bloody chunder!
jokes.setup: Physical exertion from slave labor
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg's fake Australian curse word
jokes.explanation: Mild character moment - Zoidberg's attempted tough talk with silly fake curse. Somewhat forced.

jokes.text: Don't whip with your arms. The power comes from your hips. Like this:
jokes.setup: Bender helping improve slave-driving technique
jokes.punchline: Gives professional coaching on how to torture his friends
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy - Bender's helpful nature applied to torture. Perfect character consistency and moral blindness.

jokes.text: Enoughsis! Pullutut! What's-His-Name!
jokes.setup: Bender trying to remember great pharaohs
jokes.punchline: Makes up Egyptian-sounding names ending with giving up
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay creating fake Egyptian names with escalating desperation. Good character ignorance.

jokes.text: Go faster? I told them, but they're lazy. - No. I mean, they're all free. - Freeloading off you? I agree.
jokes.setup: Pharaoh trying to free the slaves
jokes.punchline: Guard consistently misunderstands, making it worse each time
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalating miscommunication. Each misunderstanding makes the slaves' situation worse. Good structure.

jokes.text: He's whipping angels now.
jokes.setup: Announcing Pharaoh's death
jokes.punchline: Assumes cruel Pharaoh continues whipping in afterlife
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about afterlife consistency. Good economy and unexpected image of angelic torture.

jokes.text: The damned good-looking. Pharaoh commanded me to tell that joke at his funeral.
jokes.setup: Formal funeral speech about 'abode of the damned'
jokes.punchline: Terrible pun followed by excuse that dead Pharaoh wrote it
jokes.explanation: Meta-joke about bad funeral humor. The excuse for the bad pun becomes the actual joke.

jokes.text: Also this bag of cats our culture considers holy.
jokes.setup: Listing Pharaoh's burial goods
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of live cats as burial items
jokes.explanation: Absurdist visual gag about Egyptian cat worship taken to extreme. Casual delivery sells the absurdity.

jokes.text: That ends the funeral. You don't have to go home, but you can't stay here.
jokes.setup: Formal end to funeral proceedings
jokes.punchline: Priest uses bouncer cliché to end religious ceremony
jokes.explanation: Familiar reference joke - incongruous use of bar closing line at funeral. Mildly effective but predictable.

jokes.text: Free us from thought and responsibility! - We shall read things off you! - Then do them! - Your words guide us! - We're dumb!
jokes.setup: Religious ceremony consulting prophecy wall
jokes.punchline: Increasingly blunt admissions of mindless obedience
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation satirizing blind religious faith. Each line gets more brutally honest about intellectual surrender.

jokes.text: Those are waves, jackass! It's supposed to be a river!
jokes.setup: Priest misinterpreting prophecy drawing
jokes.punchline: Bender rudely corrects religious interpretation
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Bender can't help being rude even during mystical prophecy. Classic irreverence.

jokes.text: Behold! I have emerged from the place of spells and fairies!
jokes.setup: Bender claiming to be the prophesied Pharaoh
jokes.punchline: Describes bathroom with childish mystical language
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender - turns mundane bathroom break into mystical emergence. Great quotable absurdist language.

jokes.text: This society is a bunch of idiots.
jokes.setup: Everyone accepts Bender as Pharaoh based on bathroom emergence
jokes.punchline: Leela's deadpan assessment of the situation
jokes.explanation: Perfect straight-man reaction. Leela states what audience is thinking with economy and timing.

jokes.text: Let a whole new wave of cruelty wash over this lazy land!
jokes.setup: Bender promising to end the old Pharaoh's cruelty
jokes.punchline: Immediately promises new, different cruelty instead
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection - starts like benevolent ruler speech, reveals worse tyranny. Perfect Bender characterization.

jokes.text: I meant, 'Yourself to death?'
jokes.setup: Slave says he's working, Bender asks if working hard enough
jokes.punchline: Clarifies he wants them to literally work to death
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy escalation - takes workplace intensity to lethal extreme. Good economy and Bender cruelty.

jokes.text: Pharaoh, it hurts when I breathe. - Well then, what do you think you should stop doing?
jokes.setup: Slave complaining about breathing pain
jokes.punchline: Bender suggests stopping breathing as solution
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark setup-punchline. Perfect structure with Bender's logical but murderous problem-solving.

jokes.text: Are people gonna be remembering me or the statue?
jokes.setup: Massive statue built to honor Bender
jokes.punchline: Worries statue might overshadow him personally
jokes.explanation: Brilliant vanity joke - even when getting exactly what he wanted, Bender finds new insecurity. Perfect character work.

jokes.text: Too exact, if you ask me.
jokes.setup: Slaves built statue to his exact specifications
jokes.punchline: Blames them for following instructions too well
jokes.explanation: Perfect tyrannical boss behavior - blaming subordinates for doing exactly what was asked. Great character consistency.

jokes.text: Afterlife? If I had to go through another life, I'd kill myself.
jokes.setup: Discussion of afterlife and burial goods
jokes.punchline: Bender's circular logic about escaping life through suicide
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy with circular logic. Classic Bender nihilism with perfect economy and quotability.

jokes.text: Nothing can get in or out except millions of snakes.
jokes.setup: Fry saying they're sealed in the tomb
jokes.punchline: Mentions the one exception is millions of snakes
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that makes their situation worse. The casual mention of millions of snakes is nicely absurd.

jokes.text: Hey, Fry? Remember that robot, Bender? - Bender? Doesn't ring a bell.
jokes.setup: Leela testing if Fry remembers Bender while he's listening
jokes.punchline: Fry demonstrates the forgetting problem that started episode
jokes.explanation: Good callback to episode opening. Shows Bender's memory fears are justified through Fry's consistency.

jokes.text: Stop it! It's not right! You've crossed a line!
jokes.setup: Friends pretending not to remember Bender
jokes.punchline: Bender's moral outrage at fake forgetting vs. real tyranny
jokes.explanation: Great character irony - Bender finds fake forgetting more offensive than real murder. Perfect moral blindness.

jokes.text: Oh, Bender! When did you come in? Hold still.
jokes.setup: Bender finally agrees to let them blow up statue
jokes.punchline: Leela immediately tries to light him on fire
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy escalation - Leela's casual attitude toward lighting Bender on fire. Good timing.

jokes.text: You have your legacy as a brutal, tyrannical dictator... and that will outlive any monument. - You think they'll remember me? - Absolutely. Well, in that case, one planet down.
jokes.setup: Fry consoling Bender about being remembered for tyranny
jokes.punchline: Bender immediately plans to conquer more planets
jokes.explanation: Perfect ending escalation - Bender learns nothing and immediately scales up his evil. Great character consistency and dark humor.

jokes.text: Well, in that case, one planet down.
jokes.setup: Leela assures him he'll be remembered on this planet
jokes.punchline: Bender reveals he plans to conquer more planets
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender ending - his megalomania knows no bounds. Great escalation that subverts any sense of resolution.

tmdb_overview: Bender fears that nobody will remember him after he dies, and sees his chance for immortality when the crew is enslaved on the planet Osiris 4. Posing as the planet's new pharaoh, Bender orders a humongous statue built in his honor, and quickly goes mad with power.
